questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following ethers is gaseous at room temperature?
questions.solution:
Lower ethers are highly volatile and inflammable substances.
Dimethyl ether and methoxyethane are gases, whereas others are colourless liquids with pleasant odour.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Identify the major product formed when 2-Methylhexan-3-ol is heated with concentrated sulphuric acid.
questions.solution: The reaction gives more substituted alkene as a major product, in accordance with Saytzeff rule.

questions.q: Phenol does not react with $\mathrm{NaHCO}_3$ because
options: phenol is a weaker acid than carbonic acid
phenol is a stronger acid than carbonic acid
phenol is as strong as carbonic acid
questions.solution:
Carbonic acid is stronger than phenol 
Hence (A)
